AskResponse.description:
The masterpiece Ask response — the truth, sourced, with full depth.

Not a summary. Not a paraphrase. The exact sentence from the source,
with provenance you can tap to verify. PLUS: judgment, perspectives,
decision boundary, and reasoning trace from the full Core engine.

Phase 5: added counterevidence, unknowns, confidence, as_of fields
per the roadmap answer schema. The claim verifier populates
counterevidence (claims not supported by evidence) and the answer
confidence is calibrated from evidence quality.
AskResponse.title: AskResponse

BriefingResponse.description: The masterpiece briefing — Situation-centric, not agent-centric.
BriefingResponse.title: BriefingResponse

CommitmentsMasterpieceResponse.description:
The masterpiece Commitments response — one at risk, rest secondary.

Not a list of 47. One primary (the at-risk commitment), the rest
available but secondary. The inevitability: you know what you owe
without scrolling.
CommitmentsMasterpieceResponse.title: CommitmentsMasterpieceResponse

ConnectorAutoDraftRequest.description: P13 fix: only provider + recipient — commitment + evidence are DERIVED.
ConnectorAutoDraftRequest.title: ConnectorAutoDraftRequest

PrepareResponse.description:
The masterpiece Prepare response — 3 things that matter for THIS meeting.

Not 5 prep points. Three. The forgotten commitment, the open question,
the contradiction. The right three. PLUS: Cluely-class depth from
CopilotSituationBridge.pre_call_briefing().
PrepareResponse.title: PrepareResponse

TheMomentResponse.description: The single most important thing Maestro knows right now.
TheMomentResponse.title: TheMomentResponse

WhatChangedMasterpieceResponse.description:
The masterpiece What Changed response — 2 material shifts, not a feed.

Not a chronological inbox dump. Two cards. The things that materially
changed since you last looked. The inevitability: you're already
caught up.
WhatChangedMasterpieceResponse.title: WhatChangedMasterpieceResponse

info.description: HTTP API for Maestro Personal v1 — wraps the PersonalShell (Core via Python)
info.title: Maestro Personal API

delete.description: Delete the user's account and all associated data (scoped to caller).

get.description: Export all user data (GDPR/CCPA compliance).
List all wired Nerve agents.
Unified dashboard view: all insights from all agents.
Get dynamically selected agents for a situation (Directive 4).
Query a specific agent's insights.
Get ambient intelligence — what's happening between calls.

post.description: Ask a question — get the truth, sourced (LLM-powered when available).
Streaming Ask — SSE for sub-2s perceived latency.

get.description: Get the audit log — every data access event.

post.description:
Login — returns a bearer token.

P1 fix: passwordless email login removed. The login now requires
either:
1. The MAESTRO_PERSONAL_TOKEN env var (single-user local mode) —
   the caller must provide it as the password. No email-based login.
2. A per-user token that was previously created via _create_user_token.
   But tokens are never created without the setup password.

In dev mode (MAESTRO_PERSONAL_ENV not 'production'):
- Bootstrap token works with password=AUTH_TOKEN (backward compat for tests)
- Email-only login is REJECTED

In production mode:
- Only per-user tokens work (no bootstrap)
- Login requires password validation against user store (future)

This closes the P0-2 passwordless login vulnerability.

Store the user's Expo push token for sending notifications.

Issue 6: Called by the mobile app on login to register the device
for push notifications. The notification_scheduler uses these tokens
to send stale-commitment alerts.

Register a new account with email + password.

Phase 2: Real account lifecycle. Creates a user account with
a hashed password (PBKDF2-SHA256, 100k iterations). Returns
a bearer token immediately after registration.

Rate limited: 3 registrations per hour per IP.

Revoke the current token (P1-4 fix).

The caller's bearer token (from the Authorization header) is revoked.
After this call, the token can no longer be used for authentication.
The user must log in again to get a new token.

This is the standard 'logout' endpoint — it ensures that even if the
token is intercepted, it becomes useless after revocation.

Rotate the current token (P1-4 fix).

Issues a new token and revokes ALL old tokens for the user. This is
the standard token rotation flow — call this periodically to limit
the window of opportunity for a compromised token.

Returns the new token. The old token(s) are immediately invalid.

get.description: Get the user's behavior patterns for personalization.
Morning briefing — the full Situation-centric intelligence.
Evening briefing — what happened today, what's pending.
Get the Brier score + calibration report.
Get calibration history — Brier score trends over time.

Get active commitments — calls Core's commitment classifier via the shell.

DEPTH: each commitment includes calibration_note (from CalibrationPrimitives)
and outcome_history (from BehavioralLearningEngine).

schema.title: Response Get Commitments Api Commitments Get

get.description:
Read the normalized commitments ledger (Phase 3).

Returns persisted commitment entries with their lifecycle state,
owner, recipient, action, deadline, and confidence. This is the
source of truth for commitment lifecycle — the signals table holds
raw observations; the ledger holds the normalized commitments.

Filters:
  - state: filter by lifecycle state (candidate/active/at_risk/
    completed_claimed/completed_verified/disputed/cancelled/
    superseded/tombstoned)
  - entity: filter by entity name (exact match)

post.description:
Simulate the impact of taking on a new commitment.

Directive 4: 'If I take this on, what conflicts with my existing
commitments?' Analyzes deadline overlaps, entity overload, topic
conflicts, and priority dilution.

get.description:
The one commitment at risk today — not a list of 47.

The masterpiece Commitments: returns ONE primary commitment (the
most at-risk) + the rest as secondary. The inevitability: you know
what you owe without scrolling.

Phase 4: reads from the canonical WorldModel so all surfaces agree.

post.description:
Transition a commitment to a new lifecycle state (Phase 3).

The transition must be legal per the state machine. Illegal
transitions are rejected (400) AND audit-logged as
'rejected_transition'. Legal transitions are applied AND
audit-logged as 'commitment_transition'.

get.description: List all available connectors with the user's connection state.
Get the connector + draft audit log for the current user.
Google Calendar OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.
GitHub OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.
Gmail OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.
Slack OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.

delete.description: Disconnect a provider (deletes the token, keeps audit history).

post.description:
Connect a provider (stores OAuth token encrypted).

For Gmail/Slack/Calendar/GitHub: if OAuth is configured (CLIENT_ID set),
this endpoint returns the authorization URL — the user visits it, grants
access, and the provider redirects to /api/connectors/<provider>/oauth/callback
which completes the connection. If OAuth is NOT configured, stores the
provided oauth_token directly (demo mode).

P0-2 fix: the body is now optional. Callers may POST with no body at
all to initiate the OAuth flow — the previous version returned 422
because `provider` was a required body field (despite being in the URL).
Pull messages from a connector and ingest commitments as signals.

get.description: Get per-connector consent toggles for the current user.

put.description:
Update per-connector consent toggles for the current user.

Body: {"provider": "gmail", "scope": "create_drafts", "enabled": false}

post.description:
Generate a commitment-aware follow-up email draft.

Phase 5 P2: cites specific commitments + org laws. Adapts tone to
the conversation.
Get negotiation coaching from Core's NegotiationStrategyEngine.

get.description: List all playbooks (summary form).

post.description: Create or update a playbook.
Find the active playbook for the current transcript text.
Record the outcome of using a talk track (feeds the learning loop).

get.description: Get a specific playbook by ID.

post.description:
Generate a post-call summary after the meeting ends.

Phase 4: calls Core's CopilotSituationBridge.post_call_summary().
Build the full post-call summary UI payload.
Get pre-call intelligence panel: 3 things that matter for THIS meeting.

get.description: List shadow sessions (filtered by manager = current user).

post.description: Start a shadow session — manager observes a rep's live call.

get.description: Get a shadow session by ID (includes notes + feedback).

post.description: End a shadow session.
Leave structured post-call feedback for a shadow session.

get.description: List all coaching notes for a shadow session.

post.description: Add a coaching note to a shadow session.
Get talk ratio coaching from Core's TalkRatioCoach.

Transcribe an audio file using the configured STT provider.

Accepts multipart/form-data with an audio file (m4a, wav, mp3).
Returns the transcribed text, which the client then sends through
/api/copilot/transcript for real-time intelligence.

Providers (configured via env vars):
- MAESTRO_WHISPER_MODEL: local Whisper (pip install openai-whisper)
- MAESTRO_OPENAI_API_KEY: OpenAI Whisper API (cloud)
- MAESTRO_GOOGLE_STT_KEY: Google Speech-to-Text

If no provider is configured, returns 200 with configured=False
and a clear error message.

Process a transcript chunk during a live call.

Phase 4: Cluely-class real-time intelligence. Calls Core's
CopilotSituationBridge.on_transcript_chunk(). Updates the Situation's
operational state in real-time, detects new commitments, resolves unknowns.

Phase 8 fix: call detect_situations() before passing situation_id to
the copilot bridge. Without this, the situation engine is empty and
get_situation() returns None.

P1-Audit-F10 fix: auto-bind situation_id from entity when not provided.

get.description: Show which Core modules are wired to Personal (honest: producing_value vs placeholder).

post.description: Register a device for push notifications.

get.description: List drafts for the current user, optionally filtered by status.

post.description:
Create a pending draft for user approval (template formatter — P13 disclosure).

NOTE (P13): This endpoint takes caller-supplied commitment_text + evidence_refs.
It is a TEMPLATE FORMATTER, not the real capability. For the real capability
(deriving commitment + evidence from signal history), use POST /api/drafts/auto.

DERIVE a draft from the user's signal history — the real capability (P13 fix).

Only takes provider + recipient. Maestro DERIVES:
  1. The commitment (by searching the user's signals for commitments to this recipient)
  2. The evidence_refs (via keyword match + FTS5 retrieval on the recipient name)

If no commitments are found for the recipient, returns 404 with guidance.

get.description: Get a single draft by ID.

post.description:
Resolve a draft: approve (send), deny (discard), or use_draft (open in compose).

The approval flow is the trust mechanism — Maestro NEVER auto-sends.
Every draft requires explicit human approval.

get.description: Get the personal knowledge graph summary for an entity.
Get the risk prediction for a new commitment with this entity.
Health check — no auth required.

post.description:
Ingest Slack messages and extract commitments.

Directive 3: expand data sources beyond Gmail/Calendar.
Parses Slack messages, extracts commitments using the commitment
classifier, and stores them as signals.

Ingest a voice transcript and extract commitments.

Directive 3: extract implicit commitments from voice transcripts.
Processes transcript chunks, extracts commitments using voice-specific
patterns + commitment classifier, and stores them as signals.

get.description: Verify whether the Cognitive Council is LLM-powered or rule-based.
Get success metrics — tracks real user value.
Get all events for a trace ID.
Get recent traces for the authenticated user.
Get recent whisper decisions for the authenticated user.

post.description: Resolve a prediction with the actual outcome — CLOSES the learning loop.

get.description: Verify situation persistence across restart (S2 beta blocker fix).

post.description: Register a prediction — the START of the learning loop.

get.description: Get preparation for upcoming situations — 3 things that matter.

schema.title: Response Get Prepare Api Prepare Get

get.description: Get the current processing mode for privacy transparency.

Get the data retention TTL configuration (Step 15).

Returns the enforced retention periods for each data type, so users
can see exactly how long their data is kept.
Get all stored signals (scoped to the authenticated user).

schema.title: Response Get Signals Api Signals Get

post.description:
Create a new personal signal (manual entry for v1).

F4 fix: sanitizes signal text against prompt injection BEFORE storing.
S4 fix: classifies commitment type + lifecycle state on ingest using
        the LLM-powered commitment_classifier. The classification is
        stored in metadata and used by Commitments/The Moment to filter
        non-commitments (tentative/proposal/request) from active commitments.

Correct or dismiss a signal (F7 fix).

Actions:
- 'dismiss': mark signal as dismissed (removes from Moment/Commitments/Ask)
- 'complete': mark signal as completed (closes the commitment)
- 'cancel': mark signal as cancelled

The correction persists in the database.

get.description: Get all detected situations from personal signals.

schema.title: Response Get Situations Api Situations Get

post.description:
Sync Calendar events → signals.

Accepts pre-fetched calendar events. Extracts meeting.scheduled,
meeting.cancelled, and deadline.approaching signals.

Sync Gmail messages → signals.

Accepts pre-fetched Gmail messages (the OAuth wiring happens in the
mobile app or a background worker). Extracts commitments, follow-ups,
and meeting changes using the Gmail adapter.

get.description:
The single most important thing Maestro knows right now.

The Spotlight moment — the one commitment that matters most. Not a list.
If nothing deserves attention, returns has_moment=False.
Get recent meaningful deltas.

schema.title: Response Get What Changed Api What Changed Get

get.description: The 2 things that materially shifted — not a feed.

Get active whispers — things that deserve attention RIGHT NOW.

DEPTH: calls Core's WhisperSituationBridge.from_situation() for each
situation. Empty list = trusted silence.

schema.title: Response Get Whispers Api Whisper Get

post.description: Deliver high-priority whispers as push notifications.
